response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has genuine technical breadth and demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby, millions of req/s), but the resume shows no evidence of the core domain expertise this role demands: experimentation platforms, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging systems, telemetry foundations, or growth engineering. The candidate's strongest adjacent signal is low-latency microservices work, which is insufficient for a Principal-level role whose primary charter is building and owning experimentation and measurement systems. The current Naver role involves AI tooling and MCP frameworks, which is a different problem space. There is also no mention of statistical methods, metrics analysis, or cross-team platform ownership at the scale the JD requires.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency design at Dunnhumby)
Long polyglot career spanning C/C++, TypeScript, Java, and systems-level work — covers most of the JD's required language list
Demonstrated ownership and open-source platform-building (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial), showing initiative beyond day-to-day employment
concerns: Zero experimentation or A/B testing platform experience — the central technical requirement of this role is entirely absent from the resume
No telemetry, metrics infrastructure, or growth engineering background; no mention of instrumentation, feature flagging, rollout automation, or statistical analysis

Seniority gap: currently Staff-level with no evidence of Principal/IC6-scope deliverables such as multi-team platform ownership, org-wide architectural influence, or cross-functional alignment at scale
